title: costus globosus var. ridleyi
intro: Costus globosus var. ridleyi, also known as the "Malaysian Arrowroot," is a plant species within the Costaceae family, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing. The herbal preparations of Costus globosus var. ridleyi are typically made into decoctions, infusions, or poultices, which are used to treat various ailments and conditions.
